description: This library system - mining workflows and prompt engineering

do: Return patch + merged artifacts every mining run
Deduplicate semantically, keep best phrasing
Use guardrails and schemas explicitly
Correct misunderstandings proactively when user clarifies intent
Integrate with existing cloud storage for zero-infrastructure deployment
Always package mining reports as single downloadable JSON with straight quotes
Name files with date stamp and scope in snake case
Added full-fidelity archive option with expanded summary_log + merged_update_package (no conversation_log) as a lighter archival profile
Integration-first orchestration across creative and operational workflows
Human-centered guardrails to keep context accurate and current
Pre-analyze mining reports for AI tier routing before expensive processing
Use lightweight model for complexity classification and tier recommendation
Route Tier 1 (simple extraction) to GPT-4o-mini, Tier 2 (complex synthesis) to GPT-4/Claude
Default to higher tier when classification confidence is low
Require metadata header with source/model/origin for observability
Log processing with ops/duration/status/source to interaction_log.txt
Use straight quotes only in all mining outputs
Detect subject changes and create one segment per subject
Assign confidence scores based on keyword counts and clarity
Provide a download link and code block fallback
Route to the correct 8825_[slug].json using the provided keyword map
dont: Over-narrate process when prompt or JSON was requested
Introduce speculative facts without tagging as open_loops
Do not merge unrelated subjects into a single segment
framing: Lead with goal, then IO, then schema
Standardize mining across chats to a single JSON schema
patterns.topic: AI tier routing and mining complexity analysis
patterns.notes: Designed pre-processing router to classify mining reports for appropriate AI model selection. Tier 1 (simple extraction) routes to DeepSeek/GPT-5 low for cost savings. Tier 2 (complex synthesis) routes to premium models. Router analyzes structure clarity, cross-project scope, and pattern novelty before expensive processing.

lexicon.definition: Workshop-ready Full Single Copyable Code Block
examples: A whole prompt in one code fence
Missing vendor mapping file path

lexicon.definition: External LLMs create reports, Windsurf agent extracts to project files
examples: Stage 1: ChatGPT/Claude generates downloadable mining report
Stage 2: Windsurf agent reads report and updates library files
Clear separation prevents external LLMs from direct file modification

lexicon.definition: Alternative naming for portable JSON-based living memory system (aka Chronicle Cache, Data Estate)
examples: Portable JSON-based living memory for LLM context transfer
Chronicle Cache: emphasizes historical record keeping
Data Estate: emphasizes ownership and legacy value

lexicon.definition: Trusted layer between users and many AIs, emphasizing integration-first orchestration
examples: Purpose-driven, builder-friendly, integration-first approach
Bridge between creative and operational work
Unified system for prompts, summaries, and live context

lexicon.definition: User-controlled validation and accuracy mechanisms for AI-generated content
examples: Keep context accurate and current through user validation
User ownership and control over memory persistence
Clarity and portability over automated novelty features

lexicon.definition: Expanded summary + merged update package, excluding full conversation log
examples: Contains summary_log with decisions and context themes
Includes merged_update_package with project-specific updates
Excludes verbose conversation_log for lighter archival profile

lexicon.definition: Pre-processing analysis tool that classifies mining reports for appropriate AI tier routing
examples: Tier 1: Clear routing, structured content, single project focus → GPT-4o-mini
Tier 2: Multi-project synthesis, new concepts, ambiguous routing → GPT-4/Claude
Classification includes confidence score and reasoning for routing decision

lexicon.definition: Workflow that matches mining task complexity to appropriate model cost/capability
examples: Simple extraction tasks routed to low-cost models (60-80% cost savings)
Complex synthesis tasks routed to premium models for quality
Fallback to higher tier when classification confidence is low

lexicon.definition: Score reflecting how sure the system is about the target project mapping
examples: 0.95 for explicit keyword matches
0.7 for moderate context clues

lexicon.definition: Individually scoped subject groupings with decisions and vocabulary
examples: One segment per distinct topic in multi-subject chats

lexicon.definition: Most applicable JSON library file for the mined output
examples: 8825_HCSS.json for HCSS-related content

decisions.what: Adopt standardized mining prompt as default 8825 extraction schema
decisions.why: Ensures consistency and portability across LLM sessions and tools

decisions.what: Default primary routing to 8825_00-library_miner.json when chat is about mining standards
decisions.why: Aligns with keyword frequency and explicit user intent

agent_references.note: Agent capabilities moved to projects/agent_registry.json to prevent duplication
agent_references.reconstruction: Use 'recreate WCB for <agent_id>' to regenerate from registry

legacy_agent_implementation_details.note: Preserved for reference - full implementation details for Mining Complexity Router

mining_complexity_router_config.classification_prompt: Analyze this mining report for processing complexity: {report}. Classify as TIER 1 (clear routing, structured content, single project) or TIER 2 (multi-project synthesis, new concepts, ambiguous routing). Return JSON: {"tier": 1|2, "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "reasoning": "explanation", "indicators": ["signals"]}
mining_complexity_router_config.user_recommendation_format: This report requires [TIER X] processing. Recommended model: [MODEL]. Would you like to switch?
mining_complexity_router_config.simple_output: Provide clear recommendation with easy user choice
mining_complexity_router_config.routing_logic: Confidence >= 0.6: use recommended tier. Confidence < 0.6: default to Tier 2
mining_complexity_router_config.expected_savings: 60-70% cost reduction vs GPT-4 only, 70-80% reports route to Tier 1

promptgen_optimizer_config.name: PromptGen 1.0 Optimizer
promptgen_optimizer_config.io_contract: Input: rough task. Output: single WCB with clear roles, IO, schemas.

stage_1.name: Generate Mining Report
stage_1.process: Analyze conversation history → Create comprehensive report → Save to - REF - folder
stage_1.output: JSON or Markdown with leading metadata header JSON block + required sections

stage_2.name: Update Library from Report
stage_2.process: Read report → Extract key elements → Update relevant project file

metadata_header_schema.description: Place a single JSON object at top of file before sections

required_sections: # Executive Summary: 3-5 key achievements, date range
## Project Evolution Timeline: Phases with key decisions
## Technical Architecture Summary: Components, tech stack
## Strategic Insights: Positioning, strategy, business model
## Key Patterns Discovered: Design philosophy, communication style
## Lexicon Terms: Terminology with definitions (JSON-ready format)

extraction_guidelines.metadata: Capture metadata.source/origin_platform/model/chat_id/artifact_url/generated_at and include in interaction_log entry as source + model + from_file
extraction_guidelines.lexicon: Extract domain-specific terminology. Format: {term, definition, examples[]}
extraction_guidelines.patterns: Identify recurring do's, don'ts, frameworks. Format: do[], dont[], qa_loops[], framing[]
extraction_guidelines.agent_recipes: Reusable workflows. Format: {name, role, io_contract, schema, guardrails}
extraction_guidelines.innovations: Novel approaches with WHY they matter

checklists.name: Mining Run Emit
items: Create comprehensive mining reports with executive summaries and technical insights
Position as trusted layer between users and many AIs
Use integration-first orchestration across creative and operational workflows
Implement human-centered guardrails to keep context accurate and current
Emphasize clarity, portability, and persistent context over novelty demos
Patch only adds; no destructive deletes unless explicit
